question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the scaleGas() truncation to submit requests with gas values that are just below the maxGasLimit (15,000,000), causing scaleGas() to return 1,499 tokens instead of 1,500, and use the saved token to submit an additional historical-block query that would otherwise be throttled?
